references.text: Doust, A & A Drinnan. 2004. Floral development and molecular phylogeny support the generic standing of Tasmannia (Winteraceae). American Journal of Botany 91: 321-331

Marquíneza, X, LG Lohmann, ML Faria Salatino, A Salatino & F Gonzáleza. 2009. Generic relationships and dating of lineages in Winteraceae based on nuclear (ITS) and plastid (rpS16 and psbA-trnH) sequence data. Molecular Phylogenetics and Evolution 53: 435-449
Vink, W. 1970. The Winteraceae of the Old World I. Pseudowintera and Drimys - Morphology and taxonomy. Blumea 18: 225-354

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 16 June 2024

species.citation: (Hook.f.) Miers , Ann. Mag. Nat. Hist., 3rd Ser. 2 (1858) 110; ≡ Drimys piperita Hook.f. , Hooker's Icon. Pl. 9 (1852) t.896 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 154.
species.distribution: Australia, Borneo, Lesser Sunda Isls, Moluccas, New Guinea, Philippines, Sulawesi. BILIRAN, LEYTE, LUZON: Abra, Apayao (photos), Aurora (photos), Bataan, Benguet, Cagayan (photos), Camarines Sur, Ifugao, Ilocos Norte, Laguna, Mountain Province, Nueva Ecija, Nueva Vizcaya, Quezon, Rizal, Zambales, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, Bukidnon, Cotabato, Davao, Davao Oriental (photos), Davao del Sur, Misamis Occidental, Misamis Oriental (photos), MINDORO, NEGROS, PALAWAN, PANAY, SIBUYAN.
species.notes: Thirty nine local ‘entities’ recognized by Vink (mostly in New Guinea, 3 in Australia). Philippine forms not similarly characterized. Mostly in montane forests. 840-2700m.
